journal_entry.rationale: The company buys back its own shares, recording them as treasury stock, which reduces equity. Cash (an asset) decreases to fund the repurchase.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Repurchasing company stock reduces equity (Treasury Stock is a contra-equity account, increased by debit → equity decreases, slot e), funded by a decrease in cash (asset decreases → credit side, slot d).

output_credit_classifier.reason: Repurchasing company stock reduces equity (Treasury Stock is a contra-equity account, increased by debit → equity decreases, slot e), funded by a decrease in cash (asset decreases → credit side, slot d).

journal_entry.description: Refinance bank loan — old $100 note replaced by new $100 note
journal_entry.rationale: The old note payable (liability) is extinguished by debit, and a new note payable (liability) of equal value is created by credit. No cash changes hands; this is a liability-for-liability swap.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Pure liability swap — old note payable retired (liability decrease → debit slot d), new note payable created (liability increase → credit slot a). Amounts are equal so debits = credits = $100. No asset or equity accounts are affected.

output_credit_classifier.reason: Pure liability swap — old note payable retired (liability decrease → debit slot d), new note payable created (liability increase → credit slot a). Amounts are equal so debits = credits = $100. No asset or equity accounts are affected.

output_debit_corrector.reason: Pure liability swap — old note payable retired (liability decrease → debit slot d), new note payable created (liability increase → credit slot a). Amounts are equal so debits = credits = $100. No asset or equity accounts are affected.

output_credit_corrector.reason: Pure liability swap — old note payable retired (liability decrease → debit slot d), new note payable created (liability increase → credit slot a). Amounts are equal so debits = credits = $100. No asset or equity accounts are affected.

output_entry_builder.description: Refinance bank loan — old $100 note replaced by new $100 note

journal_entry.rationale: Utility expense is recognized in the current period (expense increase via debit), and since the bill has not yet been paid, a corresponding liability (Accrued Liabilities) is created (liability increase via credit).

output_debit_classifier.reason: Accrual entry — utility expense incurred but not yet paid. Expense increases (debit slot c), and a liability is created for the amount owed (credit slot a).

journal_entry.rationale: Retained earnings are reduced to reflect the dividend declared, and a liability (Dividends Payable) is created representing the obligation to pay shareholders.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Declaring a dividend reduces retained earnings (dividend increase, slot b on debit side) and creates a Dividends Payable liability (liability increase, slot a on credit side). No cash changes hands until the dividend is actually paid.

journal_entry.rationale: The typhoon-related inventory destruction is recognized as a casualty loss expense (non-operating), and the corresponding inventory asset is removed from the books at its carrying value of $2,000.

journal_entry.rationale: Cash increases by the sale price of $500 (asset up). Inventory decreases at its cost of $300 (asset down). Sales Revenue of $500 is recognized (revenue up). Cost of Goods Sold of $300 is recognized (expense up). Total debits = $800, total credits = $800.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Compound entry: Cash received at sale price ($500) increases assets; COGS at inventory cost ($300) increases expenses; Sales Revenue recognized at $500 increases revenue; Inventory removed at cost ($300) decreases assets. Gross profit = $200. Debits ($800) = Credits ($800).

output_credit_classifier.reason: Compound entry: Cash received at sale price ($500) increases assets; COGS at inventory cost ($300) increases expenses; Sales Revenue recognized at $500 increases revenue; Inventory removed at cost ($300) decreases assets. Gross profit = $200. Debits ($800) = Credits ($800).

output_credit_corrector.reason: Compound entry: Cash received at sale price ($500) increases assets; COGS at inventory cost ($300) increases expenses; Sales Revenue recognized at $500 increases revenue; Inventory removed at cost ($300) decreases assets. output_entry_builder.rationale: Cash increases by the sale price of $500 (asset up). Inventory decreases at its cost of $300 (asset down). Sales Revenue of $500 is recognized (revenue up). Cost of Goods Sold of $300 is recognized (expense up). Total debits = $800, total credits = $800.

journal_entry.rationale: The customer deposit (Unearned Revenue) was a liability — now that the service has been performed, the obligation is extinguished and revenue is recognized. No cash changes hands as payment was received earlier.

journal_entry.description: Issuance of common stock for cash
journal_entry.rationale: Cash increases (asset) by the full $180 proceeds. The credit side splits into two equity accounts: Common Stock at par value ($100) and Additional Paid-In Capital (APIC) for the excess over par ($80). Both credits increase equity.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Cash received increases assets (1 debit line). Proceeds are split between Common Stock at par and APIC, both increasing equity (2 credit lines in slot b). Total debits $180 = total credits $100 + $80.

journal_entry.rationale: The outstanding loan (liability) is extinguished and converted into contributed/share capital (equity). The liability decreases on the debit side, and equity increases on the credit side.

journal_entry.description: Conversion of preferred stock to common stock
journal_entry.rationale: Preferred share capital (equity) is reduced and reclassified into common share capital (equity). No cash changes hands — this is purely an equity-to-equity reclassification.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Equity reclassification — preferred share capital decreases (equity↓, debit slot e=1) and common share capital increases (equity↑, credit slot b=1). Total debits = total credits = $100.
